descriptionText:
Borderlands 4: Upcoming Content Roadmap

New endgame content is coming soon to Borderlands 4! Raid Boss 2 and a Takedown are on the horizon as free updates for all players, with Bounty Pack 3 dropping at the same time as the Takedown.

Borderlands 4 just hit its big Version 1.5 milestone with the March 26 launch of Story Pack 1: Mad Ellie and the Vault of the Damned, featuring the all-new Vault Hunter C4SH the Rogue and a new map zone, The Whispering Glacier. But we've still got lots more content coming your way! Here's a high-level look at what's coming in the next few months:

Update Timeline
  • Version 1.6 (April 30): Focuses on bug fixes and quality-of-life improvements, including increased spawn rates for World Bosses and World Events.
  • Version 1.7 (Late May): Introduces a free Raid Boss 2 encounter, new Pearlescent and Legendary loot, and UVHM Level 7.
  • Version 1.8 (Late June): Delivers a free Takedown (the toughest endgame yet) and the paid Bounty Pack 3 DLC.

Version 1.6 Details (April 30)
This update includes adjustments for reported issues and a close eye on C4SH the Rogue to ensure his power level is balanced. One major improvement is the increase to World Event and World Boss spawns, giving you more opportunities for exciting battles and farming dedicated drops as you traverse Kairos.

Version 1.7: Raid Boss 2 & UVHM Level 7 (Late May)
Conquer a brand-new free Raid Boss encounter for a chance at scoring new loot, including:
  • 1 new Pearlescent weapon
  • 4 new Legendary items
  • 5 new Class Mods (one per Vault Hunter)
  • UVHM Level 7: For those pushing the highest limits, Level 7 adds even further difficulty to test your skills and builds.

Version 1.8: Free Takedown & Bounty Pack 3 (Late June)
The Takedown will be Borderlands 4's most extreme endgame challenge, featuring a midway miniboss and a formidable final boss. Rewards include:
  • 1 new Pearlescent weapon & 9 new Legendary items
  • Exclusive Cosmetics: 3 Weapon Skins, 1 Global Vault Hunter Skin, and 1 ECHO-4 Drone Skin.
Bounty Pack 3 (Paid DLC): Arriving the same day as the Takedown, this pack expands the narrative on Kairos with:
  • New mission with three minibosses and a final boss
  • New Legendary & Pearlescent gear
  • Vault Card with 24 cosmetics and 4 pieces of rerollable gear
  • 1 new Vehicle, 1 Vault Hunter Skin, and 1 ECHO-4 Drone Skin

Ongoing Work & Future Features
  • Shared Character Progression Toggle: We are adding a way to toggle this off for players who want to start a new Vault Hunter with a completely clean slate.
  • Cross-Platform Saves: We remain committed to allowing you to seamlessly continue your adventure on any platform.
  • Weekly Updates: Regular refreshes for Maurice's Black Market, Weekly Activities, and balance tweaks.

Support & Community
  • If you're encountering issues, please contact 2K Support.
  • View Full Update Notes Here

Borderlands 4: 2026 Roadmap Overview

As we kick off 2026, we're excited to share what's coming to Kairos. This year, we’re focusing on major content drops while continuing to refine the base game with features like cross-save and shared account progression.
Our first Major Update of the year is now live, featuring the highly requested Photo Mode, plus balance changes and refinements.

Bounty Pack 2 & Story Pack 1
  • Bounty Pack 2: Legend of the Stone Demon (February): Includes a new mission, Legendary loot, and a themed Vault Card. Introduces Pearlescent Gear—a new rarity tier superior to Legendary.
  • Story Pack 1: Mad Ellie and the Vault of the Damned (March): Introduces a brand-new region focused on cosmic horror, new story missions, and the first new Vault Hunter: C4SH.
  • New Vault Hunter: C4SH: A former casino dealer bot turned drifter using probability-breaking Action Skills.

Upcoming Features & Endgame
  • Level Cap Increase: Future updates will increase the level cap for all players.
  • Cross-Platform Saves & Shared Progression: Seamlessly play on any platform and share SDU/Map progress across characters.
  • New Raid Boss & Takedown (Q2 2026): Formidable endgame challenges with top-tier loot.

Update Schedule
  • Major Updates (Monthly): New content, large balance changes, and performance fixes.
  • Minor Updates (Weekly): Small fixes and refreshes for Maurice’s Black Market and Boss activities.

Support & Community
  • If you're encountering issues, please contact 2K Support.
  • View Full Notes Here
